case_definition_or_reporting_change: PCR was not yet dominant; recognition, testing volume, and serology affected notifications.
notes: Use wide bounds because jurisdictional evidence documents both improved detection and false-positive serology concerns.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: After 1 Jan 2007 most notifications were PCR-based; adult serology remained common.
notes: Transition period should be treated as a reporting-regime shift rather than pure transmission.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: PCR-based notifications had largely replaced other methods by 2013, except adult serology.
notes: Modern reference regime for Australian notification data.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Confirmed cases used clinical, epidemiologic, and laboratory criteria; laboratory confirmation was a minority-to-moderate share.
notes: Clinical criteria can increase sensitivity but reduce comparability with laboratory-confirmed series.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Regional laboratories documented culture and qPCR use, but national molecular coverage was uneven.
notes: Keep prior broad because evidence is regional rather than a national laboratory denominator.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Recent regional alerts for pertussis and resistant pertussis likely increased awareness and testing.
notes: Use only as a sensitivity prior unless national testing denominators become available.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: NNDRS counts include clinically diagnosed and laboratory-confirmed cases; laboratory capacity and testing criteria varied.
notes: Pre-2017 data should not be forced onto the same observation scale as later laboratory-driven reports.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Guideline implementation requiring serology/PCR laboratory confirmation in some settings likely increased specificity and affected notification levels.
notes: Direction can vary by province; this is a national prior proxy.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Strict COVID-19 lockdowns dramatically reduced healthcare-seeking for non-emergency respiratory illness. Hospital outpatient visits dropped 40-60% nationally during peak lockdown periods.
notes: Pertussis is primarily diagnosed in outpatient settings; lockdown-driven avoidance of hospitals is the dominant explanation for the 2020 case drop.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Ongoing COVID-19 measures continued to suppress non-emergency healthcare attendance, though less severely than peak lockdown. Periodic regional lockdowns maintained reduced healthcare-seeking.
notes: Gradual recovery of healthcare attendance but still substantially below pre-COVID baseline.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Post zero-COVID policy relaxation (Dec 2022) with gradual recovery of healthcare attendance and diagnostic capacity. Pertussis awareness increasing due to rising case reports.
notes: Transition period as healthcare system returns to normal operations.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Large resurgence occurred before documented late-March enhanced active PCR screening became widespread. Heightened awareness already increasing detection.
notes: Represents early outbreak awareness with partial active screening rollout.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Multicentre evidence reports enhanced active PCR screening from late March 2024 among medically attended children. Combined with immunity debt from 3 years of reduced circulation, this produced a massive case surge. The multiplier reflects both enhanced detection AND the reporting system capturing a larger fraction of true infections than the pre-COVID passive system.
notes: This row captures the combined effect of active screening + immunity debt on reported case counts relative to the model's baseline reporting rate.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Pediatric sentinel reporting misses adult disease; NAAT was not yet widely introduced.
notes: Combines age-coverage and laboratory-method discontinuity.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: LAMP became covered by insurance in Nov 2016 before all-facility notification began.
notes: Short transition interval.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: All cases were notified from all medical facilities, with laboratory diagnosis required as a rule.
notes: Do not compare pre-2018 sentinel counts to post-2018 all-facility counts without an observation-regime term.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Pertussis became nationally notifiable; PCR diagnosis was not yet generally available.
notes: Single-year startup period.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: PCR diagnosis became available from 1997 in the context of passive national surveillance.
notes: Reference mixed diagnostic regime for historical NZ data.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: PCR is recommended; most laboratories do not offer pertussis serology, and culture is not routine.
notes: Could reduce late adult detection while improving specificity.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Reliable national burden data were limited before sentinel PCR evidence.
notes: Use as a broad passive-surveillance prior.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Sentinel hospital severe respiratory illness surveillance tested specimens by PCR; national notification remained passive.
notes: The sentinel component informs under-ascertainment but is not a direct national case-count multiplier.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Pertussis is a notifiable medical condition and PCR-based sentinel/genomic workflows are available.
notes: Recent national reports may still substantially under-ascertain non-hospitalized disease.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Post-vaccine-restart surveillance used culture/PCR/serology reporting; PCR added detections beyond culture in Swedish studies.
notes: Laboratory regime is comparatively stable but testing volume can still vary.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Established laboratory-based pertussis surveillance after the first post-vaccine decade.
notes: No strong national step-change encoded after 2008 without further testing-denominator data.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Adult pertussis was underrecognized and culture/PCR were often unavailable.
notes: Use a strong under-ascertainment prior for historical passive national data.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Narathiwat high-epidemic definition used cough >=1 week, compared with the national cough >=2 week definition, and was evaluated against RT-PCR.
notes: Geographically concentrated; do not apply nationally without down-weighting or a region-scope covariate.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Southern outbreak surveillance and modelling likely raised awareness, but national extrapolation is uncertain.
notes: Use as broad sensitivity only.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Before national serology and PCR supplementation of pertussis laboratory confirmations.
notes: Historical notification series is not directly comparable with enhanced lab era.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Serology and PCR were added nationally; adolescent/adult confirmations rose without matching hospitalization increases.
notes: This is a classic diagnostic-era discontinuity.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: Established enhanced surveillance with culture, PCR, serology, or oral-fluid confirmations.
notes: Modern reference regime for UK data.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: CSTE definition included PCR/culture, but PCR adoption and testing practice grew over time.
notes: Use broad bounds because state laboratory practices varied.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: 2014 case definition before the 2020 expansion.
notes: Relative to post-2020 CSTE definition, expected reports are slightly lower.

case_definition_or_reporting_change: The 2020 CSTE change allowed broader lab-confirmed reporting and was estimated to increase reported cases by nearly 10 percent.
notes: Important for comparisons across the 2020 boundary.
